title: rubus vestitus subsp. adscitus
intro: Rubus vestitus subsp. adscitus, also known as the thimbleberry or thimble-fruit, is a subspecies of the fragaria family, specifically a type of raspberry native to North America. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus vestitus subsp. adscitus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to support digestive health, immune function, and skin health.
